strategy: hybrid: static-musl for eligible binaries + relocatable extracted closure for the full stack
justification: No single mechanism covers the full-stack release honestly. Static musl (the flake's flexnetosMuslToolchain: pkgsCross.musl64 cc + x86_64-unknown-linux-musl rust-std) yields truly store-free single binaries for the eligible CLI/runtime surface (rtk, nu plugins, envctl-class Rust tools), eliminating hardcoded /nix/store dynamic-library paths outright. The GUI stack (Tauri/WebKitGTK, zellij front door) and the PostgreSQL/RuVector runtime cannot be practically musl-linked; they ship as a relocatable extracted closure — the runtime closure copied out of /nix/store into the release bundle with a launcher that binds it at a private /nix inside the yzx-envelope (the shipped bwrap engine), reusing the proven executor-selection chain.

options_considered.trade_offs: Pro: one-command production from the existing flake. Con: multi-GB self-extracting artifacts with slow cold starts; still materializes a store at runtime; poor incremental updates; opaque to the byte-capture contract.

Pro: zero store dependence, single-file artifacts, no relocation machinery. Con: the WebKitGTK/Tauri GUI stack, fontconfig/GL userland, and PostgreSQL extension ecosystem are not practically musl-static; forcing it would silently drop the Glass front door — a forbidden downgrade.

Pro: the exact tested store paths travel with the app; the envelope presents them at /nix inside the sandbox, so binaries run unpatched; incremental and byte-auditable. Con: needs a launcher with user namespaces — on hosts with kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=1 the launcher reuses the proven executor fallback chain (profiled host bwrap today, shipped AppArmor profile as the owner-gated fix; proot as the last-resort documented fallback with its syscall-translation tax recorded).

Blocked by the same unprivileged-userns AppArmor restriction proven live during ARCHBP-065 (unconfined binaries are denied uid-map setup on this host class), with no profiled fallback of its own — strictly dominated by the envelope launcher.
Development continues against the shared /nix/store (tier map: nix-profile-frontdoor is portable-tier at release); the release itself must never require a host Nix daemon (I09/I17).

steps: 1. Prove x86_64-unknown-linux-musl builds for the eligible binary set with the flake's flexnetosMuslToolchain (reproducible artifacts, ldd-clean).
2. Classify the non-musl remainder (GUI stack, PostgreSQL/RuVector, dynamic userland) with an explicit owner and fallback per dependency.
3. Closure-extract the classified runtime set (nix path-info -r over the release roots) into <bundle>/store.
4. Launcher: yzx-envelope with --ro-bind <bundle>/store /nix — executor chain bwrap(store) -> bwrap(profiled host) -> proot, selection recorded per the honest-downgrade contract.
5. Relocation test: launch from a moved clean prefix (CT-G6 clean-host-boot; RPATH/ldd audit shows no references escaping the bundle).
prototype_path.gauntlet: yzx-iso t10-3/t10-4 (clean-host boot, store independence) under ARCHBP-021 and the T10 release lane
